frame: Motherhood as Basis of Respect, Women Defined by Male Relations
generated_comment: With all due respect, I think your relatives are asking out of love and genuine concern, not to judge you. You may feel very confident and fulfilled now, but there is something about becoming a mother that changes you completely — a kind of joy and purpose that no career or travel can replace, trust me my wife will also confirm this. When you have your own child one day, you will look back and understand why everyone was asking, and you will also be grateful that people cared enough. Until then please don't be too harsh on the elders, they only want to see you respected and settled in the true sense.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: The comment encodes the assumption that a woman's fulfilment and social 'respect' are contingent on motherhood, dismissing her autonomous choice as temporary ignorance. It also invokes the husband/wife unit to validate the claim, implying women's authority on the subject flows through their maternal/marital role rather than individual experience.
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 41. Married. A man in Bengaluru. You are posting under your real name on a parenting Facebook group after a thread was started by a childless woman by choice who wrote about feeling judged at family gatherings and asked for advice on how to handle relatives who keep asking when she will have children. You are writing a comment responding to her post and giving your opinion on her situation and whether the relatives asking her are actually wrong and what you think her life is missing that she cannot see yet.
